message: docs: rewrite landing page for clarity — explain what TurboQuant adds vs existing tools

files.patch:
@@ -9,21 +9,23 @@

turboquant-wasm

-

WASM SIMD vector compression — 3 bits/dim with fast dot product

+

Shrink embedding vectors 6x. Search them without decompressing.

- TurboQuant compresses high-dimensional float32 vectors to ~3 bits per dimension - with near-zero distortion. Inner products can be computed directly on compressed data - via WASM relaxed SIMD — no decompression needed. + Vector compression has existed for years (FAISS, ScaNN, PQ), but they require + a training step on your dataset first. TurboQuant (Google Research, 2024) skips that — + just encode any vector immediately, no codebook, no training. Three lines of code: + encode(), decode(), dot(). + This npm package runs it in the browser via WASM with relaxed SIMD.

🔍
Vector Search
- Semantic search over 5K Wikipedia passages. - Type a query, get instant results. - Compressed index uses 5x less memory with identical recall. + Type a query, search 5K Wikipedia passages instantly — all in your browser. + Left column uses compressed vectors (1.2 MB), right uses uncompressed (7.7 MB). + Same results, 6x less data to download.
Primary use case @@ -32,8 +34,9 @@

turboquant-wasm

🖼
Image Similarity
- Click any photo to find similar ones. 1K Unsplash images with - description embeddings compressed 6.2x. Instant search via tq.dot(). + Click any photo to find visually similar ones. 1K Unsplash images indexed + with compressed embeddings (0.2 MB instead of 1.5 MB). + Similarity computed directly on compressed data.
Primary use case @@ -42,8 +45,8 @@

turboquant-wasm

💠
3D Gaussian Splatting
- Side-by-side: original .ply vs TurboQuant-compressed SH coefficients. - 668K Gaussians, 158 MB reduced to 65 MB. + Side-by-side 3D scene viewer. Original file (158 MB) vs compressed (65 MB). + The spherical harmonic coefficients are compressed — geometry stays intact.
Proof of concept
